title: The Tale of the Talking Gluten-Free Broccoli
summary: Hattie the mouse visits Minnie and Mickey Mouse at their home, where they discover a very unusual surprise in their refrigerator: a talking piece of broccoli! Because the broccoli doesn't want to be eaten, the friends decide to have a dinner of apples and chicken nuggets instead. In a surprising twist, the broccoli joins them at the table and requests a pear for its own meal. After a busy evening of feeding the pet kittens and getting plenty of rest, the group spends the next day playing games and singing together.

highlightQuote.text: Because they have gluten-free broccoli and it doesn't talk. Gluten-free broccoli doesn't talk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, are you all ready for Stars and Stories? Here we go. Turning out the lights. Here they come. Stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Always about Hattie the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stars and Stories, always about Hattie the mouse. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse visited the deep deep dark.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not the deep deep dark.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No? Okay, Hattie visited...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No no. No we were just telling the story about Mickey and Minnie with Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, let's continue that one. So, Minnie Mouse brought little Mickey back home from school, and Hattie the mouse came along to their house. And they came inside and Hattie noticed their kitchen, which had vegetables in it, and what else was in the kitchen?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out, right. So there were spatulas in the kitchen, and forks and knives and spoons, and apples and bananas and pears, and cups and bowls and plates, and a sink...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What else?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And a rhinoceros, and a chair...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. No, not a rhinoceros.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh you're right, there wasn't a rhinoceros. But there was a dishwasher, and there was a refrigerator. And what was in the refrigerator?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Vegetables.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And there was something funny in the refrigerator. What was it? What was it that was the funny thing in the refrigerator that didn't belong in the refrigerator?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was bro...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. No, not broccoli. You keep telling story and then you find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, well it was broccoli, but it was special funny talking broccoli. And Minnie the mouse opened the refrigerator and she was just saying like, 'Okay, I wonder what we should have for dinner,' and when she opened the fridge, suddenly the broccoli said, 'Not me, not me!' And Minnie said, 'Who was that talking?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That was broccoli. So close the fridge up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh I'll close it. And then...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And you can just have some apple for dinner or maybe some chicken nuggets.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, apple or chicken nuggets, that sounds good. Does everybody want that? And what did Mickey Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh we do!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But Hattie said, 'Oh I like broccoli. Do you think we could have some broccoli though? Do you have any?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Because we can get more at the store that doesn't talk. Because they have gluten-free broccoli and it doesn't talk. Gluten-free broccoli doesn't talk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, said Hattie mouse, I see. Okay, well I'll have to wait until next time and we can get some gluten-free broccoli at the store cause it doesn't talk, that's a good point.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But the store's open now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well that's okay, we can just have apple and chicken nuggets. And Minnie Mouse said, 'Yeah, that's a good idea.' And so she cooked everyone some delicious dinner and they all sat down to eat it. And what did they talk about while they ate their dinner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Broccoli.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They talked about broccoli, cause it was kind of surprising that the broccoli was talking. And did they invite broccoli to come sit at dinner with them to talk?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did the broccoli say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The broccoli said, 'Yes, I would like to come to dinner.' And Minnie the mouse asked the broccoli, 'Oh hi broccoli, what would you like to eat for dinner?' And what did the broccoli say to that question?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But I was trying to ask you what you want to eat, broccoli.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Broccoli said, 'I want to eat a pear.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A pear? Oh that sounds good. You know, now that you mention it, I'd like to have some pear too. And Hattie said, 'Yeah me too.' And Mickey said, 'Me too.' And so Minnie Mouse went and cut up a pear and served it for everyone and each person got a few little pear slices on their plate, and the broccoli did too. And the broccoli went, 'Nom nom nom, that's some delicious pear. Thanks for serving me some pear even though I'm just a talking broccoli.' And then they were all done with dinner and they cleaned everything up. And then they had to feed Minnie and Mickey's pets. Who do they keep as pets in their house?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Simps, Ginger, and Garfield.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Ginger and Garfield, what kind of animals are they?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They're kittens.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh they're kittens. And what did they eat for dinner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They ate kibble and wet food.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Kibble and wet food, that sounds like a nice dinner for cats. And then what did everyone do after dinner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They went to sleep. Zzzzz.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then they woke up the next morning and what did they do the next day?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They played and played and played and played and played. Yay!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mickey Mouse said, 'Hey everyone, let's sing a song.' And Minnie said, 'Let's see, what songs do we know how to sing?' And what did Mickey say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars and Stories done. I want Stars and Stories to be done.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh you want Stars and Stories to be done? Okay, we can be done. Oh, let's turn out the lights.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they're having game that you really want now... and I hope you see it now...

characters.name: Hattie the mouse
characters.description: A sociable companion to Mickey and Minnie, this mouse finds joy in gathering friends over hearty pots of homemade stew or setting off on spirited explorations, such as her recent journey through the shimmering wonders of a deep-sea aquarium.

characters.name: Mickey Mouse
characters.description: Minnie’s musical companion and star-bound co-pilot is an industrious toy mouse who returns from school to mend the broken and fill the silence of the void with song. While he remains Izzy’s imagined confidant—a silent listener to her whispered anxieties regarding dwindling supplies—he also finds joy in companionship, proudly showcasing his intricate Lego creations to a trusted friend amidst the stars.

characters.name: Minnie Mouse
characters.description: A gracious mouse and tireless caretaker who bridges the gap between her charming storefront, rugged construction sites, and the shimmering cosmos. Whether she is curating fine art supplies, clearing giant mud puddles as an expert digger driver, or piloting her vibrantly colorful rocket ship through the stars, she tends to her community with a unique blend of delicate charm, heavy-duty utility, and an explorer’s boundless spirit.

characters.name: Talking Broccoli
characters.description: A surprising vegetable found in the refrigerator who talks but doesn't want to be eaten, preferring to join the group for a pear dinner.

characters.name: Simps
characters.description: One of Minnie and Mickey's pet kittens.

characters.name: Ginger
characters.description: A soft, round, and silver-coated feline, this exceptionally fluffy educator is one of Minnie and Mickey’s own kittens grown into a figure of quiet authority. Known for daintily distributing napkins with velvet paws and commuting to school behind the wheel, the gray-furred teacher balances his role on Nana’s welcoming committee with a life of polite, helpful service. Whether presiding over his humble sanctuary—the very cage Seeker once coveted—or sustaining himself on crunchy kibble and savory wet food, he remains a steady presence, most recently seen trading his classroom duties for the shadows of a secret closet hideout alongside Garfield.

characters.name: Garfield
characters.description: Minnie and Mickey’s intrepid kitten is a seasoned morning commuter, navigating the school run to reach Seeker’s sanctuary for heavy napping and premium kibble. A social fixture known for sharing twilight dinners with Ginger or waiting expectantly at Nana’s for a warm welcome, they occasionally trade their public life for the quiet solitude of a closet, sequestering themselves away with a treasured hoard of toys.

places.name: The Kitchen
places.description: The castle’s grand culinary hall, where Minnie and Mickey orchestrate legendary feasts, is a whimsical blend of stone-walled majesty and cozy clutter. Gleaming utensils and vibrant vegetables spill across the counters, all centered around a peculiar, animated refrigerator that hums with a personality as lively as the banquets prepared within.

places.name: The Store
places.description: A versatile apothecary and provisioner where Seeker procures heavy-lidded sleep potions and Hattie guides Victor toward his exhaustive list of non-hat essentials. The eclectic inventory doubles as a grocery for all sizes, where local mice browse for carrots alongside a larder of gluten-free broccoli prized for its refreshing silence. Tucked among these oddities are the Band-Aids that once stirred a flicker of temporary concern in Izzy.
